kings_guild.name: The King's Guild
kings_guild.description: Master artisans of the King's Guild each ply a different trade in a different quarter of the city. Work out who's who.
clues: The Cooper's levy is at least 5 coins away from the artisan serving the Earl.
Rowan's levy is between the Carpenter and the artisan with the Saw.
Godfrey goes with at least two of Blacksmith, Hammer, Knight, and 7 coins.
Either Cooper goes with Edmund or someone in the Market Ward goes with Earl (but not both).
Hammer goes with exactly two of Godfrey, Weaver, Highrow, Bishop, and 7 coins.
Saw goes with Eastcheap.
Between Blacksmith and Carpenter, one goes with Needle and the other with Duke.
Exactly one member of the Clothiers' Guild is in the River Ward.
Edmund, Blacksmith, Needle, and Bishop belong to different artisans.
Edmund's levy is higher than both the Weaver and the artisan with the Needle.

fishing.name: The Fishing Derby
fishing.description: Anglers at the lake-country derby each landed a different fish from a different pond on a different lure. Work out who caught what, and where.
clues: The angler who landed the Pike's weight is at least 6 lb away from the angler fishing with Maggot.
Wade's weight is between the angler who landed the Catfish and the angler using the Swimbait.
Esther goes with at least two of Bass, Popper, Nightcrawler, and 6 lb.
Either Pike goes with Boone or someone in the East Watershed goes with Maggot (but not both).
Popper goes with exactly two of Esther, Trout, Foxglen, Corn, and 6 lb.
Swimbait goes with Birchpool.
Between Bass and Catfish, one goes with Spoon and the other with Dough.
Exactly one member of the Pike Family is in the North Watershed.
Boone, Bass, Spoon, and Corn belong to different catches.
Boone's weight is higher than both the angler who landed the Trout and the angler using the Spoon.
